isolation.notes: All project commands executed via nix develop -c ...; negative cases used -o paths inside the evidence directory so output absence could be checked safely.

assertions.title: `use net::http` introduces a usable stdlib package namespace

steps.action: Compile the positive namespace fixture through rgoc.
steps.expected: Compilation succeeds and writes Go output.

steps.action: Inspect the generated Go and run downstream Go checks.
steps.expected: Go output imports net/http and passes gofmt -d, go build, and go vet.
steps.observed: Generated Go imports "net/http" and contains http.NewServeMux, http.ListenAndServe, http.ResponseWriter, and *http.Request; gofmt -d, go build, and go vet all exited 0.

steps.action: Use the qualified positive fixture as the control case.
Compile a fixture that calls new_serve_mux() without the http:: qualifier after use net::http.
steps.expected: Compilation fails with an unresolved-name diagnostic and leaves no output file.
steps.observed: Exited 1 with `undefined name 'new_serve_mux'`; outputs/unqualified_call.go was not created.

assertions.title: Imported stdlib symbols resolve coherently in both type and value positions

steps.action: Compile a fixture that uses http::ResponseWriter and http::Request in a handler signature while also calling http::new_serve_mux and http::listen_and_serve.
steps.expected: The same program compiles through rgoc and downstream Go build succeeds.
steps.observed: Compilation exited 0 and downstream go build/go vet both exited 0.

assertions.title: Missing imports fail with package-aware diagnostics

steps.action: Compile a fixture using http::new_serve_mux/http::listen_and_serve without use net::http.
steps.expected: Compilation fails with a package-aware diagnostic and no Go output.
steps.observed: Exited 1 with `package 'http' is not imported; add 'use ...::http;' at the top of the file`; outputs/missing_import_call.go was not created.

steps.action: Compile a fixture using http::ResponseWriter and http::Request in type position without the import.
steps.expected: Compilation fails with the same package-aware diagnostic and no Go output.
steps.observed: Exited 1 with `package 'http' is not imported; add 'use ...::http;' at the top of the file`; outputs/missing_import_type.go was not created.

assertions.title: Unknown stdlib members fail with member-aware diagnostics

steps.action: Compile a fixture that references http::missing_symbol after a valid use net::http import.
steps.expected: Compilation fails with a member-oriented diagnostic and leaves no output file.
steps.observed: Exited 1 with `undefined member 'missing_symbol' in imported package 'http'`; outputs/unknown_member.go was not created.

assertions.title: Malformed import syntax is rejected cleanly

steps.action: Compile representative malformed imports: single-segment `use http;`, trailing `use net::http::;`, and slash-form `use net/http;`.
steps.expected: Each fixture fails deterministically with syntax/import diagnostics and leaves no output file.
steps.observed: The single-segment form failed with `malformed import: expected 'use pkg::sub'`; the trailing and slash forms failed with syntax errors; none of the requested output files were created.

assertions.title: Imported namespaces do not silently collide with local names

steps.action: Compile a fixture that imports net::http and also declares a local fn http().
steps.expected: Compilation fails with an explicit collision diagnostic rather than silently resolving to the wrong namespace.
steps.observed: Exited 1 with `import alias 'http' collides with an existing declaration`; outputs/collision_fn.go was not created.

assertions.title: Non-stdlib package paths fail explicitly until external packages are implemented

steps.expected: Compilation fails with an explicit external-package-not-supported diagnostic and no Go output.
steps.observed: Exited 1 with `unsupported external package 'github::gin'`; outputs/external_package.go was not created.

assertions.title: Stdlib call sites use snake_case while stdlib type names stay PascalCase

steps.action: Compile a representative fixture that combines http::listen_and_serve, http::new_serve_mux, mux.handle_func, http::Request, http::ResponseWriter, and http::ServeMux.
steps.expected: Compilation succeeds and the generated Go lowers to http.ListenAndServe, http.NewServeMux, mux.HandleFunc, *http.Request, and http.ResponseWriter.
steps.observed: Compilation exited 1 with `no method 'handle_func' on type http::ServeMux`; outputs/positive_bridge.go was not created.

assertions.issues: The directional naming bridge works for package-level functions and PascalCase Request/ResponseWriter types, but mux.handle_func is not implemented on http::ServeMux, so the representative source-level contract cannot be satisfied yet.

steps.action: Compile a wrong-case callable fixture using http::NewServeMux().
steps.expected: Compilation fails with a clear wrong-case missing-symbol diagnostic and no output file.
steps.observed: Exited 1 with `wrong case: 'http::NewServeMux' should be 'http::new_serve_mux' ...`; outputs/wrong_case_callable.go was not created.

steps.action: Compile a wrong-case type fixture using http::response_writer/http::request.
steps.expected: Compilation fails with a clear wrong-case missing-symbol diagnostic and no output file.
steps.observed: Exited 1 with `wrong case: 'http::response_writer' should be 'http::ResponseWriter' ...`; outputs/wrong_case_type.go was not created.

assertions.title: Import, naming, and stdlib type usage compose coherently in one program

steps.action: Compile a single program that imports use net::http and combines package-qualified snake_case calls with PascalCase stdlib types in the same file, including mux.handle_func.
steps.expected: Compilation succeeds and generated Go shows the lowered names composing together.
steps.observed: Compilation exited 1 with `no method 'handle_func' on type http::ServeMux`; outputs/positive_bridge.go was not created.

assertions.issues: The same program composes package-level net/http names and Request/ResponseWriter types successfully only until mux.handle_func is introduced; at that point rgoc rejects the program because http::ServeMux has no handle_func method.

frictions.description: Every nix develop invocation printed the flake shell banner and a dirty-tree warning, so gofmt cleanliness had to be confirmed by the absence of any diff after the banner rather than by empty stdout.
frictions.resolution: Used the saved gofmt log together with the generated Go file inspection; the log contains only the shell banner/warning and no diff hunks.

frictions.description: The tuistory shell session reported `clear: xterm-truecolor: unknown terminal type`, which made initial terminal snapshots noisy.
frictions.resolution: Regenerated the terminal snapshots using raw ANSI clear sequences before each log view.

summary: Tested 11 assertions on the rgoc CLI/generated-Go surface: 9 passed and 2 failed. The failures were VAL-STDLIB-001 and VAL-CROSS-001 because a representative program using mux.handle_func is rejected with `no method 'handle_func' on type http::ServeMux`, while the rest of the import-surface contract behaved as specified.
